1.description: nomic-embed-text-v1.5 is a text embedding model based on a long-context BERT architecture, trained with unsupervised contrastive learning on weakly related text pairs. It supports a maximum sequence length of 8192 tokens and produces 768-dimensional embeddings using Matryoshka Representation Learning, allowing flexible dimensionality reduction with minimal performance loss. The model is now multimodal, aligned with nomic-embed-vision-v1.5.

2.description: A lightweight sentence transformer model that maps sentences and short paragraphs into a 384-dimensional dense vector space. Designed for semantic search, clustering, and sentence similarity tasks with efficient inference capabilities.

3.description: This sentence-transformers model maps sentences and paragraphs to a 768-dimensional dense vector space for tasks such as clustering and semantic search. It encodes short paragraphs and sentences, truncating input text longer than 384 word pieces by default. The model was fine-tuned on over 1 billion sentence pairs using a contrastive learning objective on TPU v3-8 hardware with a sequence length limited to 128 tokens during training.

4.description: EmbeddingGemma is a 300M parameter text embedding model from Google, built on Gemma 3 with T5Gemma initialization. It generates 768-dimensional vector representations of input text, supporting tasks like search, retrieval, classification, clustering, and semantic similarity. Trained on data in over 100 languages, it has a maximum input context length of 2048 tokens. Matryoshka Representation Learning enables truncation to 512, 256, or 128 dimensions.

5.description: Snowflake/snowflake-arctic-embed-l is a text embedding model with 335 million parameters and 1024-dimensional output vectors, optimized for high-quality retrieval performance. It supports a maximum input sequence length of 512 tokens and is designed primarily for English-language tasks, though part of a suite including multilingual variants.[1][2]
valid_prefixes: Represent this sentence for searching relevant passages:

task_map.query: Represent this sentence for searching relevant passages:

6.description: BGE-M3 is a multilingual embedding model supporting over 100 languages. It handles dense retrieval, multi-vector retrieval (ColBERT), and sparse retrieval. The model processes inputs from short sentences to long documents up to 8192 tokens, with an output embedding dimension of 1024.

7.description: BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5 is a BERT-based text embedding model producing 768-dimensional vectors for English text. It supports a maximum input sequence length of 512 tokens and is designed for dense retrieval tasks like semantic search and passage retrieval. The model outputs normalized embeddings suitable for cosine similarity computations.[1][2][4][6]

8.description: intfloat/e5-large-v2 is a text embedding model with 24 transformer layers that produces 1024-dimensional vectors. It requires inputs prefixed with 'query: ' or 'passage: ' and uses average pooling over the last hidden state followed by L2 normalization. Long texts are truncated to 512 tokens maximum. This model is designed for English text retrieval tasks, achieving strong performance on benchmarks like MTEB and BEIR.[1][2][7]

9.description: The gte-large-en-v1.5 model is a text embedding model built on a transformer++ encoder backbone combining BERT, RoPE, and GLU. It supports a maximum sequence length of 8192 tokens and produces 1024-dimensional embeddings. With 434 million parameters, it achieves state-of-the-art performance on the MTEB benchmark (65.39 score) and competitive results on LoCo long-context retrieval tests, primarily optimized for English text with reported multilingual capabilities.
